items.title: Cross-entropy H(P, Q)
items.description: BCE and CE are supervised uses of H(P, Q) = −Σ p log q. The canonical definition, Shannon derivation, and decomposition H(P, Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P || Q) live in Cross-entropy — link there rather than duplicating the information-theory treatment here.

items.title: Regression vs Classification
items.description: Problem type (continuous y vs discrete y) determines which loss family applies. This topic supplies the loss formulas; Regression vs Classification covers target types, output heads, and when each supervised setting applies.

items.title: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
items.description: Minimizing average BCE or CE equals maximizing Bernoulli/Categorical log-likelihood. Minimizing MSE on Gaussian targets relates to MLE of the conditional mean. MLE explains why these losses are the default training objectives.

items.title: RMSE & MAE
items.description: RMSE and MAE are common regression evaluation metrics on held-out data. MSE and MAE here are training losses with the same core formulas; RMSE & MAE covers metric interpretation, scaling, and reporting — not re-derived here.
